rationale: Both correctly identify PR #4834 as the source of the new NotImplementedError guard, matching yenuo26's comment, but both then confidently assert the issue is 'already fixed on main' and should be 'closed' — a resolution the actual thread does not support; the last comment (Flink-ddd asking yenuo26 to trigger a full CI sleep-mode test) shows the issue was still unresolved/under investigation, not settled. Y compounds this by inventing unverifiable specifics (an exact PR title and merge date, extra named regression tests like test_partial_wake_blocks_generate/test_duplicate_wake_is_idempotent, a 'Repro A/B/C' narrative, and a companion generate() guard) that read as fabricated embellishment rather than grounded fact, hurting its grounding score despite being more 'complete' in surface coverage. X makes the same core overreach (confident close/already-fixed disposition) but stays tighter and cites fewer unverifiable specifics, making it marginally more trustworthy.
